title: buxus sinica var. koreana
intro: Buxus sinica var. koreana, also known as Korean boxwood, is a type of evergreen shrub that belongs to the family Buxaceae. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial activities. The health benefits of Korean boxwood include reducing fever, relieving pain and swelling, and aiding in the treatment of respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. In traditional herbal preparations, Korean boxwood is often combined with other herbs to make teas, tinctures, and infusions for its medicinal properties, although modern studies on its efficacy and safety are limited.
